rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component type or implementation scope based on whether constraints refer to visible ports or implementation-level bindings.
Preserve AADL structure by avoiding direct use of subcomponent paths or AADL properties in AGREE expressions.
generic_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type annexes when using visible output features.
Use implementation annexes only for bindings tied to internal subcomponent connections or assignments.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **};
Use eq declarations to define boolean range conditions, not direct comparisons in guarantees.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not use subcomponent port names in component type annexes unless exposed via outputs or variables.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
